rationale: In the General Discussion, the authors explicitly cite Dhar and Simonson (2003) when arguing that forced-choice tasks can steer shoppers toward options with less conflict/regret. This citation is used to interpret why their lab (forced-choice) and field (free-choice) results may differ, rather than as a generic literature-list mention. While it is not a core methodological foundation of the paper, it has a clear conceptual function in explaining design-related outcome differences.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Dhar and Simonson's forced-choice insight to interpret its own cross-study pattern. Specifically, it uses the idea that forced choice shifts focus toward low-conflict options to explain differences between forced-choice lab tasks and a free-choice field task.
